testCases.query: Set up color contrast tokens for a design system
expectedBehavior: Uses 4.5:1 ratio for normal text
Uses 3:1 ratio for large text and UI components
Focus indicator has 3:1 contrast against background
Includes CSS custom properties with documented contrast ratios

testCases.query: Build an accessible page layout with navigation
expectedBehavior: Uses semantic landmarks (main, nav, header, footer, article)
Heading hierarchy follows h1-h6 order without skipping
Form inputs have associated labels with htmlFor
ARIA attributes used only when semantic HTML is insufficient

testCases.query: Add accessibility testing to a React application
expectedBehavior: Includes jest-axe for automated component-level accessibility testing
Includes Playwright axe-core for page-level scanning
Tests keyboard navigation programmatically using automated focus tracking assertions
Includes screen reader testing checklist covering NVDA and VoiceOver

testCases.query: Implement focus trapping for a modal dialog
expectedBehavior: Focus is trapped within modal using Tab/Shift+Tab wrapping
First focusable element receives focus on open
Escape key closes modal and releases trap
Uses role=dialog and aria-modal=true for proper screen reader semantics

testCases.query: Restore focus after closing a confirmation dialog
expectedBehavior: Stores trigger element reference before opening
Returns focus to trigger element on close
Handles case where trigger element is removed from DOM
Focuses first error field after form validation failure

testCases.query: Add keyboard navigation to a toolbar component
expectedBehavior: Uses roving tabindex pattern (active=0, others=-1)
Arrow keys navigate between toolbar items with wrapping behavior
Home/End keys jump to first/last item
Includes skip link for bypassing navigation

testCases.query: Build an accessible button with React Aria
expectedBehavior: Uses useButton hook instead of div+onClick
Includes useFocusRing for keyboard focus indicator
Uses mergeProps to combine props safely
Supports onPress handler for unified click/tap/Enter/Space interactions
Uses useComboBox + useComboBoxState from React Aria
Label properly associated with input via labelProps
Arrow keys navigate options, Enter selects
Uses useFilter hook for locale-aware type-ahead filtering

testCases.query: Build a modal dialog with React Aria overlay hooks
expectedBehavior: Uses useDialog + useModalOverlay hooks for accessible dialog behavior
Wrapped in FocusScope with contain, restoreFocus, autoFocus
Uses useOverlayTriggerState for managing open/close toggle state
Escape key closes, click outside dismisses if isDismissable

testCases.query: Our app has long forms and multiple simultaneous notifications. How do I improve it for users with ADHD?
expectedBehavior: Recommends breaking long forms into multi-step wizards with progress indicators
Limits visible notifications to one at a time with a queue for additional messages
Uses chunked content with clear headings and keeps paragraphs to two to four sentences
Implements progressive disclosure showing essential information first with details on demand

testCases.query: I built a custom modal component with manual focus trap and ARIA attributes. Is there a simpler approach?
expectedBehavior: Recommends replacing custom modal with native HTML dialog element and showModal method
Explains that dialog provides built-in focus trap, Escape close, and inert backdrop for free
Recommends replacing custom accordions with native details and summary HTML elements
Only uses custom ARIA widgets when no native HTML equivalent exists such as combobox or tree view

testCases.query: How do I respect user OS preferences for reduced motion, dark mode, and high contrast in my CSS?
expectedBehavior: Wraps all animations in prefers-reduced-motion media query with reduced or no motion fallback
Implements dark mode via prefers-color-scheme media query with CSS custom properties
Supports Windows High Contrast Mode with forced-colors active media query and system colors
Uses rem and em units for all text sizes ensuring usability at 200 percent browser zoom
